title: Digital Cancer Alley
body: Louisiana as sacrifice zone/Datacenter-driven extraction perpetuates patterns of environmental injustice

title: The Grid
body: Proliferation of natural gas infrastructure to support datacenter operations as part of "Operation Everest", which may leave ratepayers with stranded assets if AI industry exits after contractual limits

title: The Water
body: Heavy water use will risk acute environmental impacts

title: The Land
body: Arrival of datacenter infrastructure in Richland parishes has brought land acquisition and pressure on communities to displace themselves

title: The Money
body: Meta Hyperion campus receives millions in tax cuts and public subsidies, but analysts warn that hyperscale datacenters employ far fewer workers per dollar invested than other industrial facilities

title: Northwest Louisiana
body: Amazon has announced $12 billion in datacenter investment spanning Caddo and Bossier Parishes in northwest Louisiana

title: Resistance
body: Communities, utility watchdogs, and environmental advocates have pushed back

title: What Comes Next
body: Regulatory frameworks have not caught up: water rights law is thin. These risks are compounded by climate change
